Bash(gh pr create --title "Replace Docker with PM2 for market-maker deployment" --body "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## Summary
- Add PM2 ecosystem config for `bebop` and `relay` processes \(auto-restart, memory limits, log rotation\)
- Add `scripts/deploy.sh` for one-command deploys: `git pull → yarn build → pm2 restart`
- Add `scripts/setup-server.sh` for first-time server provisioning \(Node 22, PM2, repo clone, systemd startup\)

## Why
Docker is overkill for two Node processes on a $6/mo droplet — rebuilds take ~2 min \(re-downloads all deps\), eats memory on a 1GB box. PM2 deploys in seconds \(`git pull && yarn build && pm2 restart`\) with less overhead and built-in crash recovery.

## Test plan
- [ ] Run `scripts/setup-server.sh` on a fresh Ubuntu droplet
- [ ] Verify `pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs` launches both services
- [ ] Verify `pm2 startup` + `pm2 save` survives reboot
- [ ] Run `scripts/deploy.sh` to confirm one-command redeploy works
- [ ] Verify pricing stream shows `✓ N valid options` in `pm2 logs bebop`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code]\(https://claude.com/claude-code\)
EOF
\)")
